use_case_per_ir8477_section3.target_audience: WIDAI framework developers, cybersecurity workforce planners, and stakeholders evaluating how WIDAI's data/AI workforce taxonomy relates to the U.S. government's cybersecurity workforce standard. Secondary audience: organizations building bridge programs between cybersecurity and data/AI career paths.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.why_this_mapping: NICE is the model WIDAI emulates structurally — both use Task, Knowledge, and Skill building blocks organized into work roles. This STRM serves three purposes: (1) validate WIDAI's KSA taxonomy design against the framework it was modeled after, (2) identify governance, risk, and management competencies that bridge cybersecurity and data/AI workforce domains, (3) map the overlap boundary between cybersecurity and data/AI to inform career pathway development. NICE is Tier 1 Priority 2 — the structural validation framework after O*NET's breadth validation.

focal_document.exclusion_rationale: No elements excluded. Unlike O*NET where physical/psychomotor/sensory abilities were categorically irrelevant, NICE TKS elements span a continuum from purely tactical cyber operations to governance and management. Pre-filtering would introduce subjectivity. All 2,148 TKS elements are evaluated; those outside WIDAI scope produce 'No relationship' results that document the scope boundary.

concept_types_mapped.structural_note: NICE v2.1.0 uses TKS (Task, Knowledge, Skill) building blocks. The original SP 800-181 (2017) used KSA (Knowledge, Skills, Abilities); Revision 1 (2020) dropped Abilities as a separate element type and elevated Tasks to building-block status. WIDAI retains Abilities as a distinct type. This structural difference means NICE has no direct Ability-type elements to compare against WIDAI Abilities — but NICE Skills and Tasks may functionally overlap with WIDAI Abilities.

use_case_per_ir8477_section3.direction_of_mapping: Focal → Reference. Each NICE TKS element (focal) is evaluated against the WIDAI KSA pool (reference). A NICE element with 'No relationship' to any WIDAI KSA indicates either (a) a cybersecurity-specific competency outside WIDAI scope, or (b) a potential gap in WIDAI coverage.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.exhaustiveness_level: Exhaustive. Every NICE TKS element (2,148) is evaluated. For each element, the most semantically relevant WIDAI KSA is identified via bi-encoder candidate screening followed by semantic analysis. Where multiple WIDAI KSAs relate to a single NICE element, the strongest relationship is recorded as primary with secondary candidates noted in rationale.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.rationale_type: Semantic (primary). NICE and WIDAI use different vocabularies — NICE uses cybersecurity-centered language ('cybersecurity policy', 'system security posture') while WIDAI uses data/AI-centered language ('data governance policy frameworks', 'AI risk quantification'). The conceptual overlap is real but the terminology diverges. Semantic rationale evaluates whether differently-worded concepts describe overlapping competencies. Functional rationale is used as secondary for Task elements where the operational outcome matters more than vocabulary similarity.
use_case_per_ir8477_section3.rationale_justification: NICE is a cybersecurity workforce framework. WIDAI is a data and AI workforce framework. The domain overlap exists in governance, risk management, compliance, privacy, architecture, leadership, and workforce management — areas where both disciplines share competency requirements. Semantic rationale is necessary because the same underlying competency (e.g., 'risk management') is expressed through domain-specific vocabulary in each framework.

scope_decisions.note: All 2,148 TKS elements evaluated without categorical exclusions. The 428 'No relationship' results are analytically valuable — they map the boundary between cybersecurity and data/AI workforce competencies.

structural_elements_excluded.work_roles: 41 work roles are structural containers, not FDEs. Work roles group TKS elements; the TKS elements themselves are the mappable concepts.
structural_elements_excluded.categories: 5 work role categories (OG, DD, IO, PD, IN) are organizational groupings.
structural_elements_excluded.competency_areas: 11 competency areas are thematic groupings, not individual competency statements.
structural_elements_excluded.opm_codes: 40 OPM codes are federal classification identifiers.

candidate_screening_methodology.description: Due to the volume of elements (2,148 vs O*NET's 126), a two-stage evaluation approach was used: (1) Bi-encoder embedding (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) computed cosine similarity between each NICE element and all 497 WIDAI KSAs, identifying top-5 candidates per element. (2) Keyword-domain matching classified elements by scope overlap, with semantic rules determining relationship type and best-match assignment.
candidate_screening_methodology.note: The bi-encoder stage is a screening tool, not the final scoring method. Final strength scores are computed by the STRM scoring pipeline (Cross-Encoder STS primary + 4 secondary methods), identical to STRM-001-ONET.
